citation.description: Three chip anchors href='#gate-plan', '#gate-completion', '#gate-code' with verdict + count spans; backed by test_audit_ticket_page_heldout.py::test_gate_strip_shows_all_three_states_counts_and_anchors

criteria.criterion: The sticky gate-status strip shows all three gate verdicts with magnitude counts simultaneously, each chip anchoring to its detail section. Oracle: an E2E test on a ticket passing plan review (blocking=0), failing completion (k/n met), and with no code review, asserting the rendered HTML shows all three states + counts + anchor hrefs.

citation.description: ticket.html includes gate sections in immutable order: plan(_gate_section), completion(_completion), code(_gate_section); backed by test_sections_render_in_fixed_order

criteria.criterion: Detail sections render in the fixed order plan → completion → code regardless of which gate failed. Oracle: an E2E test comparing section order across two tickets with different failing gates.

citation.description: _finding.html renders pass-1 (finding/criteria/location/evidence), pass-2 (verification dict), pass-3 (decision+reason for plan), pass-4 (coaching); backed by test_plan_finding_expands_to_four_pass_values

criteria.criterion: A PLAN-review finding row expands in place to the four-pass provenance (Finding / Verification / Decision / Coaching). Oracle: an E2E test seeding a finding with known, distinct field VALUES and asserting the expanded HTML contains the SPECIFIC values — the pass-1 evidence string, the pass-2 verification value, and the pass-3 decision + reason string. A code-review finding row renders its available fields without asserting an absent pass.

citation.description: Meter rendered from f.meter_fill_pct (priority) and f.meter_tick_pct (block_threshold) with f.meter_class color; v1 fallback 'threshold not recorded (v1)'; backed by test_threshold_meter_distinct_ticks_per_finding and test_threshold_meter_v1_fallback_when_threshold_absent

criteria.criterion: The threshold meter renders a bar filled to priority with a tick at the finding's OWN resolved block_threshold (a per-finding value, not a round-level scalar), with a decision-series-derived fill color class. Oracle: two E2E tests — distinct tick positions for two findings with different thresholds, and v1 fallback caption.

citation.description: _group_and_sort orders four SERIES with block open=True and rest open=False; sorted by priority descending; _gate_section.html renders <details open> for blocking group; backed by test_findings_grouped_by_decision_blocking_expanded

criteria.criterion: Findings are grouped by decision series with BLOCKING expanded and ADVISORY / DROPPED / INDETERMINATE collapsed-and-counted, sorted by priority within each group. Oracle: an E2E test asserting group order, expanded blocking rows, and correct collapsed counts.

citation.description: conv.form=='bars' → div.conv-bars with one div.conv-bar per round; else svg.conv-line with one polyline per non-empty series; sr-only table always present; _convergence() in page.py skips polylines for empty series; backed by test_convergence_two_round_bars and test_convergence_four_round_line_with_polylines

criteria.criterion: The convergence visualization renders stacked per-round bars (div.conv-bars with one .conv-bar per round) for a ≤3-round ticket, and a line graph (svg.conv-line with one polyline per non-empty decision series) for a ≥4-round ticket, each with an accompanying visually-hidden screen-reader data table. Oracle: two E2E tests.

citation.description: select_round() clamps to 1 (latest); _selector() builds older_href/newer_href with independent gate params; _gate_section.html renders round selector with links; backed by test_round_selector_default_latest_and_older

criteria.criterion: The ◂ round n of N ▸ selector lists the retained plan- and code-review rounds newest-first, defaults to the latest, and selecting an OLDER round (via ?<gate>_round=<n>) server-renders that round's distinct content (findings + convergence). Oracle: an E2E test seeding ≥2 rounds, asserting the selector shows N, defaults to latest, and requesting an older round renders its distinct findings.

citation.description: PASS path: crit-row with glyph, badge, citation, lacking flag (kind==operator-attested and not met); FAIL path: 'FAIL — failing findings' h3 heading; backed by test_completion_pass_criteria_and_operator_attested_flag and test_completion_fail_renders_failure_findings_fallback

criteria.criterion: The completion panel renders one chevron-less row per criterion from a PASS verdict's criteria[] (met glyph + kind badge + citation) and flags an operator-attested criterion lacking an attestation; for a FAIL verdict (no criteria[]) it renders the failure findings under an explicit FAIL heading. Oracle: two E2E tests.

citation.description:
When section.ran is False renders
Not yet run.
inside
with

heading; same in _completion.html; backed by test_plan_only_ticket_shows_not_run_panels

criteria.criterion: A ticket with no code review and no completion verdict shows an explicit plan-review-only page with real h2/h3 'not yet run' panels for the absent gates. Oracle: an E2E test asserting the empty-state panels render with headings.

citation.description: Tests are present and well-formed; page.py (395 LOC) and server.py (140 LOC) are both under 500-LOC module-size policy; imports are clean; type annotations are consistent. Cannot run commands but no static red flags detected.

criteria.criterion: `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
